data.title: Arribatec boosts treasury holdings with NOK 13m share buyback
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Arribatec Group ASA ( (DE:B7Z) ) has shared an announcement.

Arribatec Group ASA has repurchased 2,000,000 of its own shares at NOK 6.50 per share in a block sale facilitated by Pareto Securities, for a total consideration of NOK 13 million. Supported by a prior buyback mandate and a strong liquidity position, the board viewed the transaction as an attractive opportunity, increasing the company’s treasury shareholding to 2,148,459 shares and signaling confidence in its valuation and capital allocation strategy.

The buyback, executed on 13 March 2026 with settlement due on 17 March 2026, underscores Arribatec’s active use of share repurchases as a tool for balance sheet optimization and potential shareholder value enhancement. The move may improve earnings per share over time by reducing the free float and could strengthen the company’s positioning in the market as it continues to invest in growth across its international software and consulting operations.

More about Arribatec Group ASA

Arribatec Group ASA is an Oslo-headquartered software and consulting company that focuses on a customer-centric engagement model, leveraging deep system, integration and domain expertise to build long-term strategic partnerships. The company serves more than 1,700 customers across 25 countries in both private and public sectors, employs over 320 people in 11 countries, and is listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange under the ticker ARR.

Average Trading Volume: 102,013

Current Market Cap: NOK588.4M

data.title: Fujikura to Book $200 Million Dividend from U.S. Subsidiary as Non-Operating Income
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fujikura Ltd ( (JP:5803) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Fujikura Ltd. said it will receive a dividend of 200 million U.S. dollars, equivalent to about 31.5 billion yen, from its consolidated subsidiary America Fujikura Ltd. in March 2026. The payment, drawn from retained earnings, underscores the subsidiary’s profit-generating capacity and provides a sizable cash inflow to the parent.

The company will book the dividend as non-operating income in its non-consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. Fujikura expects only minimal impact on its consolidated results, highlighting that the transaction is largely an internal capital reallocation rather than a change in the group’s overall earnings power.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:5803) stock is a Buy with a Yen25103.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fujikura Ltd stock, see the JP:5803 Stock Forecast page.

More about Fujikura Ltd

Fujikura Ltd., listed on the Prime Market of the Tokyo Stock Exchange under code 5803, operates in the electrical and electronics sector, historically focusing on products such as cables, wiring solutions, and related components. The company serves global markets through subsidiaries including America Fujikura Ltd., reflecting a broad international footprint and diversified earnings base.

Miramar Resources Limited has issued 894 million unquoted options exercisable at $0.004 and expiring on March 13, 2028, as notified in an Appendix 3G to the ASX. data.title: Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund hit by Middle East turmoil but adds to Asian tech bets
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Ltd. ( (AU:FPC) ) has provided an announcement.

Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund reported an estimated pre-tax net tangible asset value of $1.9869 per share as at 11 March 2026, down from $2.1916 on 28 February, representing a 9.34% decline. The manager attributed the drawdown to the market turmoil sparked by the Middle East conflict, which has weighed on its core positions in precious metals, Japanese financials, and Chinese technology, though the fund’s shares remain cum a 2 cent special dividend.

Management expects the conflict to keep markets unsettled in the near term but anticipates a relatively swift resolution and a subsequent rebound, drawing parallels with last year’s tariff-related sell-off and recovery. Despite sector-wide multiple compression and investor concerns over AI disruption, the fund notes relative resilience in U.S. technology stocks and has selectively increased exposure to major China and Asian tech names such as Tencent, Baidu, Alibaba, Tencent Music, GDS Holdings and Grab, signalling confidence that technology valuations may be near a bottom.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:FPC) stock is a Buy with a A$2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Ltd. stock, see the AU:FPC Stock Forecast page.

More about Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Ltd.

Fat Prophets Global Contrarian Fund Limited is an Australia-listed investment company trading on the ASX under the code FPC. The fund follows a contrarian global equity strategy, with core thematic exposures in precious metals, Japanese financials and banks, and Chinese and broader Asian technology stocks, aiming to capitalise on cyclical and policy-driven market dislocations.

Average Trading Volume: 29,477

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Janison moves to reassure after unrelated glitches hit NAPLAN and CA ANZ exams
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Janison Education Group Ltd. ( (AU:JAN) ) has provided an announcement.

Janison Education Group has responded to recent media coverage after two separate technology interruptions disrupted some digital assessment sessions for NAPLAN Online and Chartered Accountants Australia and New Zealand candidates. The company expressed regret for the impact on students, teachers, schools, families, and candidates, and said it is working with program partners to review the incidents as part of its continuous improvement efforts.

The NAPLAN disruption on 11 March 2026 halted a portion of morning sessions but was resolved within about two hours, allowing testing to resume the same day and continue without further issues. A separate issue affected CA ANZ exams, where some candidates faced interruptions and are being offered rescheduled sessions, with Janison stressing that these are the first major problems in five years of running CA ANZ exams and that the two affected platforms are distinct, reinforcing its broader record of reliable large-scale delivery.

The company highlighted that it delivers more than 7 million digital assessments annually in complex, multi-partner environments where occasional issues can arise despite extensive testing. It reaffirmed its commitment to reliable digital assessment delivery, established contingency arrangements for disrupted tests, and close collaboration with partners, seeking to reassure stakeholders about the robustness of its systems and its focus on safeguarding the integrity of key education programs.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:JAN) stock is a Hold with a A$0.21 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Janison Education Group Ltd. stock, see the AU:JAN Stock Forecast page.

More about Janison Education Group Ltd.

Janison Education Group is an Australian-owned education technology company that provides online assessment solutions to governments, education systems, professional bodies, and corporations worldwide. Its platforms power large-scale, high-stakes programs such as NAPLAN Online, enabling millions of digital assessments each year with a focus on reliability, accessibility, and fair testing at scale.

Average Trading Volume: 54,408

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$38.98M

For a thorough assessment of JAN stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: 3P Learning Director Katherine Ostin Increases Indirect Shareholding via On-Market Purchase
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

3P Learning Ltd. ( (AU:3PL) ) has shared an update.

3P Learning has disclosed a change in director Katherine Ostin’s indirect holdings in the company, lodged in accordance with ASX listing rules on director interests. The notice details that Ostin’s interest is held via Nitso Pty Ltd as trustee for the Ostin-Shortus Family Trust, where she is both a director of the corporate trustee and a beneficiary.

On 12 March 2026, Ostin acquired 22,988 fully paid ordinary shares in 3P Learning through an on-market trade at $0.435 per share, increasing her indirect holding from 11,111 to 34,099 shares. The company confirmed there was no trading during a closed period requiring prior written clearance, indicating the transaction complied with relevant governance and disclosure obligations for directors’ dealings.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:3PL) stock is a Hold with a A$0.46 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on 3P Learning Ltd. stock, see the AU:3PL Stock Forecast page.

The company provides online educational programs, including mathematics and literacy platforms, targeting school-aged students and educational institutions. Its products are delivered as fully paid digital services, positioning it within the broader edtech and online learning market.
